agent_output:

{

This is NOT an infrastructure change — it is a fundamental alteration to the platform's compliance posture, contractual obligations, and business value proposition.",

      { "term": "Statutory Retention", "definition": "Minimum data retention periods imposed by law — tax, customs, consumer protection, and commercial regulations." },

      "invariant_justification": "Statutory requirement across EU jurisdictions. Multi-market operation means the longest applicable retention period governs unless data is jurisdiction-partitioned.",

      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Proof of delivery records must be available for the duration of the applicable limitation period for legal claims. In the UK this is 6 years (Limitation Act 1980); in most EU jurisdictions 3-6 years.",

      "invariant_justification": "Without PoD, the company and its customers cannot defend against claims of non-delivery, damaged goods, or incorrect delivery. Destroying evidence within the limitation period is legally indefensible.",

    },

      "id": "BR-6",

      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "GDPR is often misinterpreted as 'delete everything ASAP.' In reality, GDPR requires proportionate retention — deleting data you are legally required to keep is itself a compliance failure. This is a policy interpretation, not a hard invariant.",
      "testable_assertion": "Data retention periods MUST be justified by documented lawful basis under GDPR. Statutory retention obligations constitute valid lawful basis and MUST NOT be overridden by a blanket 90-day deletion policy."
    }
